english: mtso-rláṅs (mtsho rlangs) vapours, mtso-rlábs (mtsho rlabs) waves of a lake. — *tso-lág* (mtsho lag) C. inlet, creek, cove. — *to-lag-dél* (to lag del) C. strait, channel.
Lt., also mtsog-ysėn (mtshog gsen) Cs., 'spot or tender part of the head', vacancy in the infant cranium, = tsáṅs-pai bú-ga (tshangs pa'i bu ga).

adv., *tsógs-se* (tshogs se) adj., W. for mtsuṅs (mtshungs) or odra ('dra), similar, like, equal; *aṅ-ré-zi tsogs rgyál-la mi dug* (ang re zi tshogs rgyal la mi dug) they are not so good as the English; *ko daṅ ṅá-la dug-ṅál tsóg-se yod* (kho dang nga la sdug bsngal tshogs se yod) with him and with me there is the like disaster, misfortune visits us equally.

1. also mtsón-ča (mtshon cha), any pointed or cutting instrument, mtsón-čas ytúb-pa (mtshon chas gtub pa) to cut to pieces with such an instrument Dzl.; weapon, arms; mtson togs-pa (mtshon thogs pa) to seize a sword, to take up arms Dzl.; mtsón-gyis ojig-pa (mtshon gyis 'jig pa) to destroy, to conquer, with the sword Ma.; mtsón-ča rnám-pa bźi (mtshon cha rnam pa bzhi) Stg.: sword, spear, dart, arrow; go-mtsón (go mtshon) armory and arms; ru-mtsón (ru mtshon) v. ru (ru); mtson-krág (mtshon khrag) blood drawn by cuts or stabs (used for sorceries) Lt. — mtson-gyi dru-bu (mtshon gyi gru bu) an attribute of the gods, resembling a coil or ball of thread Wdn.; mtson-skúd sgril-ma (mtshon skud sgril ma) Thgr. id. (?). — 2. also tson (tshon) fore-finger, mtsón-rtsa (mtshon rtsa) the pulse to be felt with the fore-finger; mtson gaṅ (mtshon gang) a finger's breadth; mtson gaṅ mar (mtshon gang mar) a finger's breadth lower Med.; mtsón-pa (mtshon pa) a four-fingers' pinch (?); śiṅ mtsón-pa źig (shing mtshon pa zhig) a handful of sticks Mil.

1. v. mtson (mtshon). — 2. vb. to set forth, bring forward, adduce, state, quote, exhibit, examples of grammatical forms etc. Gram.; odis mtsón-nas ('dis mtshon nas) illustrating it by this, setting this up as an example Gram.; des kyaṅ sgyú-mai dpe čig mtson (des kyang sgyu ma'i dpe cig mtshon) also in this may be seen an instance of deception Mil.; dpes mtsón-pa (dpes mtshon pa) to illustrate by parables Mil.; mtsán-mas (mtshan mas) by a sign Gram.; so prob. also: ăm-ban rnyis dei mtsón-pai dmág-mi (am ban gnyis de'i mtshon pa'i dmag mi) the soldiers brought forward by the two Chinese officials; it is also alledged to stand for to make, to prepare C. — rnám-mka mtsón-pai rnál-obyor-pa (rnam mkha' mtshon pa'i rnal 'byor pa) prob.: the saint that represents the heavens, that resembles the heavenly space Mil.

1. vb., pf. tsags (tshags), btsags (btsags), fut. btsag (btsag), imp. tsog (tshog) (trans. to odzag-pa ('dzag pa)), to cause to trickle, to strain, filter, sift, squeeze, press out, bru-már tság-pa ('bru mar tshag pa) (partic.) oil-miller Dzl.; to draw off, dmú-ču (dmu chu) to tap (a dropsical person) S.G. Cf. tság-ma (tshag ma), tsags (tshags). — 2. adj. thick, fat, obese Lex.

fault, error, offence, sin, de otsáṅ-du če (de 'tshang du che) that is very wicked, a great offence; mii (mi'i) or mi-la otsaṅ brú-ba (mi la 'tshang 'bru ba) or drú-ba ('dru ba) 1. to spy out another's faults, to upbraid him with them, to accuse him Do., C., W.; *tsaṅ og dhú-wa* (tshang 'og 'dru ba) C. id. — 2. to irritate, provoke, make angry C.

vb. I. pf. tsaṅs (tshangs), fut. btsaṅ (btsang) (?) 1. to press into, to stuff Sch., otsáṅ-ka byéd-pa ('tshang ka byed pa) id. Sch.; náṅ-du otsáṅs-pa (nang du 'tshangs pa) Lexx. prob. pressed into, stuffed inside, so Sch.: kri naṅ tsáṅs-čan (khri nang tshangs can) a stuffed seat; dbugs kar otsáṅs-pa (dbugs khar 'tshangs pa) out of breath, panting (in the heat of pursuit) Mil.; dbugs stod-du otsaṅs-nas skad mi ton (dbugs stod du 'tshangs nas skad mi thon) Mil. I am pressed for breath, my breath stops, I cannot utter a word (for ardent longing); stod-otsáṅs (stod 'tshangs), rluṅ-otsáṅs (rlung 'tshangs), otsáṅs-la pan ('tshangs la phan), all these expressions imply a want of breath, not sufficiently to be reconciled to the original meaning of the word. — 2. *sú-la tsáṅs-se yóṅ-če* (su la tshangs se yong ce) Ld. to attack a person with open violence, opp. to a stealthy attack. — II. pf. saṅs (sangs), which verb, however, occurs only in otsaṅ-rgyá-bar ogyúr-ba ('tshang rgya bar 'gyur ba) to become Buddha Dzl. frq., otsaṅ rgyá-bar odód-pa ('tshang rgya bar 'dod pa) to aim at Buddha-ship, and saṅs-rgyás (sangs rgyas) (having become) Buddha. Besides this form, there exists also a verb sáṅ-ba (sang ba), pf. (b)saṅs ((b)sangs), to clean, as may easily be proved by examples. The whole will perh. become clear, if we presume, that the form otsaṅ-ba ('tshang ba) for the present tense is now obsolete, occurring only in reference to Buddha, as quoted above, and that the root saṅ (sang) is now used as present tense in the following significations: 1. to remove (impurities) — like odág-pa ('dag pa) — to make clean,
